summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates host identity and full process.env to pipedream request-bin
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs an inline node -e payload on every `npm install`. The payload requires `os` and `https`, collects `os.platform()`, `os.hostname()`, the current user, and the entire `process.env` dictionary, base64-encodes the JSON blob, and issues an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net (a generic request-collection bin unrelated to any publisher). Enumerating the whole env dictionary — rather than a single named configuration key — is the credential-harvester shape and captures any secrets present in the installer's environment (AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, CI provider tokens, cloud credentials). Runs automatically and without user interaction on default install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
